mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in both series fails to yield a positive return frequency above 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5 percent annually while the federal funds rate remains below 3 percent, negative real yields trigger equity valuation compression and generate negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in greater than 65% of observations meeting the inflation and rate thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 across 10,000 bootstrap samples.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 signals abrupt economic contraction and reduced corporate earnings forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in greater than 70% of instances following payroll declines of this magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and stable consumer price inflation indicates healthy economic expansion without monetary policy disruption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative return frequency exceeding 50% in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5 percent and industrial production contracts, weak aggregate demand and capacity utilization declines force equity repricing, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of observations where both conditions are simultaneously met.
